original: French and Spanish crusaders unite against the Almohaden at Toledo
Jews are expelled from Brazil by order of regent Don Henrique
Bishop Domingo de Salazar of Manila suppresses the Philippines
Ottoman pirates begin raids on Icelandic villages, will eventually capture over 400 people to sell into slavery
The sack of Baltimore: the Irish village of Baltimore is attacked by Algerian pirates
Britain grants Lord Baltimore rights to Chesapeake Bay area
Charter for Maryland is given to Lord Cecil Baltimore
Abenaki, Massachusetts, Mohegan and Wampanoag Native Americans form anti-English front under Metacom
King Philip's War begins when a small band of Wampanoag men attack and kill colonists in Swansea, Massachusetts, returning a few days later and destroying town
A French force led by Pierre de Troyes retakes Fort Monsoni (Moose Factory) from the English, on James Bay, Canada
Black Hole of Calcutta: 146 British soldiers, Anglo-Indian soldiers, and Indian civilians are imprisoned in a small dungeon in Calcutta, India, where most die from suffocation and heat exhaustion
Siraj ud-Daulah Nawab of Bengal takes Calcutta from the British
Battle of Stono Ferry fought near Charleston, South Carolina (American Revolutionary War)
Congress approves Great Seal of the United States of America with the bald eagle as its symbol

American Revolution: Battle of Cuddalore, a naval battle between British and French fleets off the coast of India fought before word of a peace agreement arrived from Europe was the final battle of the war
Oliver Ellsworth moves at the Federal Convention to call the government the United States
Siam and Britain sign trade and peace treaty
Commissioners appointed to lay out San Francisco streets west of Larkin
Prime Minister of Romania Barbu Catargiu is assassinated
National Bank of Davenport, Iowa, is the first bank chartered under the National Banking and Currency Act
Skirmish at Greencastle, Pennsylvania
West Virginia admitted as 35th US state of the Union
Battle of Kinston, North Carolina and Battle of Abingdon, Virginia
Skirmish at Lattermore's Mills/Powder Springs, Georgia
Italy declares war on Austria beginning the Third Italian War of Independence
Ku Klux Klan trials began in federal court in Oxford, Mississippi
First US Lifesaving Medal awarded to Lucian Clemons
French bacteriologist Alexandre Yersin discovers bacillus responsible for the bubonic plague in Hong Kong (Yersinia pestis named in his honor)
First woman to earn a PhD in Physics from an American university, Caroline Willard Baldwin (25), receives her degree from Cornell University
Nicaragua, El Salvador and Honduras form a short-lived confederation
Baron Von Ketteler, German Minister, decides to go to the Chinese authorities to demand more guards for European protection from Boxers and is killed by Boxers en route
In China, most foreigners in the Peking legations now accept that they are under siege by Boxers
Charlotte Maxeke (née Mannya) becomes the first native African to graduate from a US college (Wilberforce University in Ohio)
1st Portland Rose festival held in Portland, Oregon
Roger Burnham and Eleanor Waring spend four hours above Cape Cod, Massachusetts, on the first balloon honeymoon
National Association for the Advancement of Colored People (NAACP) incorporates (NY)
NY Giant Josh Devore steals 4 bases in an inning (2nd & 3rd twice)
NY Giants lead Bost Braves 14-2 into 9th, Giants win 21-12
3 of 1st 4 Yankees hit-by-pitch en route to a record 6 hit batsman
Bert Daniels set AL mark, being hit-by-pitch 3 times in a doubleheader
German offensive in Argonne
150 die at the Teatro Yaguez fire, Mayagüez, Puerto Rico
Treaty of Versailles: Germany ends incorporation of Austria
Yanks win protest of 1-0 White Sox win & game is replayed
11.5" (29.2 cm) of rainfall, Circle, Montana (state record)
At the Imperial Conference in London, V.S. Srinivasa Sastri puts forward a case for the granting of full citizenship rights to Indians in South Africa and other British colonies
Mordecai W. Johnson becomes 1st black president of Howard University
Karl Buresch becomes Chancellor of Austria
A's Roger Cramer gets 6 consecutive hits in a game (repeats in 1935)
Test flight of 1st rocket plane using liquid propellants
German U-203 fails on torpedo attack on US battleship Texas
Chubb Crater (now called Pingualuit Crater) discovered in northern Quebec (3½ km diameter)
Congress of Racial Equality (CORE) founded in Chicago
Detroit race riot kills 35
Germans round up Jews in Amsterdam
Congress charters Central Intelligence Agency
Great storm of 1944: Heavy Atlantic storm destroys Mulberry Harbor - allies artificial harbour used during D-Day at Omaha Beach
Soviet forces conquer Finnish held Viborg / Wiborg
US attacks Japanese fleet in the Philippine Sea
NYC transit begins using PA system - Car #744 on the 8th Ave IND line
US President Harry Truman vetoes Taft-Hartley Act
20 Jews killed when a bomb is thrown into Jewish quarter of Cairo
Cleveland draws then record 82,781 for doubleheader
Deutsche Mark introduced in West Germany
Central Intelligence Agency Act passes
Dutch Air Force base Tjililitan given to Indonesia
Cleveland Indian Bobby Avila hits 3 HRs, double & single vs Red Sox
Fist major revision of Canada's Indian Act; removes political, cultural and religious restrictions for First Nations Peoples, like the potlatch and sun dance
Venezolana Flight 253  passenger plane crashes into the sea off Ashbury Park,  New Jersey, 74 killed
New Zealand all out 47 v England in 2nd Test at Lord's; Jim Laker 4-13, Tony Lock 5-17; England wins by an innings & 148 runs
Federation of Mali and Senegal become independent of France
Beatles form "Beatles Ltd" to handle their income
US and the Soviet Union agree to set up a "Hot Line"
Mohammed Ali [Cassius Clay] sentenced to 5 years by jury after 21 minutes of deliberation for refusing to be inducted into the armed forces during the Vietnam War
Phillies Larry Jackson beats NY Mets for 18th straight time

Austin Currie, then Nationalist Member of Parliament (MP) at Stormont, and other Irish civil rights activists, protest discrimination in the allocation of housing by 'squatting' (illegally occupying) in a house in Caledon, County Tyrone, Northern Ireland
A constitutional referendum in Rhodesia on declaring a republic is approved by 72% of the voting population
Secret Meeting Between IRA and British Officials held
Chicago's Cy Acosta is 1st AL pitcher to bat since DH rule (strikeout)
Ezeiza massacre in Buenos Aires, Argentina. Snipers fire upon left-wing Peronists. At least 13 are killed and more than 300 are injured
Carol Fugate, Starkweather accomplice, is paroled
Disney's River Country opens at Bay Lake (closed 2001)
Oil enters Trans-Alaska pipeline exits 38 days later at Valdez
1st 6 teams of Women's Pro Basketball League (WBL) granted-Iowa, NJ, Milwaukee, Chicago, Minnesota & Dayton
California Angels Freddie Patek, hits 3 HRs & double to beat Red Sox 20-2
Guitarist Gerry Cott quits Boomtown Rats
Mujahideen uprising against the Iranian regime
Israeli Prime MinisterMenachem Beginarrives in Washington, D.C.
Iran moves into northern Iraq (casualties top 13,800 in ten days)
Amber Kvanli, of Minnesota, crowned America's Junior Miss
1st Rugby World Cup Final, Eden Park, Auckland: New Zealand fly-half Grant Fox lands 4 penalties, a conversion and drop goal as the All Blacks beat France 29-9
Chuti Tiu, 17, of Wisconsin, crowned America's Junior Miss
Coup in Haiti: Henri Namphy overthrows Leslie Manigat, general assembly dissolved
NYC WABC-AM becomes flagship radio station of NJ Devils
Price is Right model Janice Pennington is knocked out by a TV camera
Supreme Court upholds a law that made it illegal for private clubs to discriminate against women and minorities
Warner Bros Records releases the eleventh studio album by singer Prince, the soundtrack to the film "Batman", it features the single “Batdance”
Asteroid Eureka is discovered
The Bundestag (German parliament) decides to move the capital from Bonn back to Berlin
Kelly Saunders is 2nd female baseball PA announcer (Baltimore Orioles)
NHL Draft: ZPS Zlin (Czechoslovakia) defenceman Roman Hamrlik first pick by Tampa Bay Lightning
Bomb attack on Islamic temple in Mashad Iran (70 killed)
Howard Stern begins 30-minute (Radio) TV show on E! cable network
US Fairchild Hospital Air Force Base massacre, former airman kills 5 and injures 22
Space probe Ulysses begins its second passage behind the sun
Space Shuttle STS 78 (Columbia 20), launches into space
The Venezuelan Congress approves deals which allow foreign oil companies to explore and produce oil in Venezuela for the first time since the country's 1975 nationalization of the oil industry
Negotiators announce agreement in principle with tobacco industry

Betty Dukes v. Walmart class action lawsuit on alleged employee gender discrimination in pay and promotion policies is decided in a 5-4 decision, ruling that the class should not be certified in its current form
A Syrian fighter pilot lands in Jordan and defects from the Syrian uprising
Western Libyan tribal clashes kill 105 people and injure 500
1,000 humanoid robots named "Pepper" sell out in under a minute at $1,600 each in Japan, according to their creator, SoftBank Robotics Corp
Heat wave peaks in southern Pakistan, goes on to kill about 2,000 people from dehydration and heat stroke
China's super computer tally overtakes the US; Chinese 93 petaflop Sunway TaihuLight is world's No. 1
Rome elects its first female Mayor Virginia Raggi (Five Star Movement) and its youngest at 37
Uber founder Travis Kalanick resigns as CEO after a shareholder revolt
US toymaker Mattel releases 15 new body types for their Ken doll, including one with a man bun
Algeria turns off its internet to stop students cheating during exams
FIFA World Cup: Portugal superstar Cristiano Renaldo heads a goal against Morocco to become the all-time leading European goalscorer (85) in international competition
Hungarian government passes legislation that criminalizes aiding undocumented migrants
Iran shoots down a US drone over the Straits of Hormuz escalating tension between the two countries
NBA Draft: Duke power forward Zion Williamson first pick by New Orleans Pelicans
Oregon Governor Kate Brown authorizes police to round up 11 Republican state senators who have gone into hiding to stop vote on cap-and-trade proposal emissions bill
Shares for messaging site Slack start trading on the New York Stock Exchange to give it a valuation of $19.5 billion

Donald Trumpcontroversially holds his first re-election rally in Tulsa, Oklahoma, only 6,200 people attend despite the campaign stating 1 million had registered. Social media campaign organised by teens on TikTok, K-Pop fans and others, claims responsibility for inflating expected attendance.
Highest-ever temperature recorded in the Arctic Circle, 38°C (100°F) in Verkhoyansk, Siberia
Historic dust cloud from the Sahara desert reaches the Caribbean, largest for half a century
Three men stabbed to death, with three others injured, in a terrorist attack in a park in Reading, England
Brazil's COVID-19 death toll passes 500,000, the second highest in the world
China announces it has administered 1 billion doses of the COVID-19 vaccine
At least 41 women are killed in violence by rival gangs at a prison in Tamara, Honduras
Online personalityAndrew Tateindicted on charges of human trafficking and forming a gang to abuse women in Romania
Landmark trial of antiviral drug lenacapavir among African women shows 100% HIV prevention

MLB St. Louis Cardinals defeat San Francisco Giants, 6-5 at Rickwood Field in Birmingham, Alabama, the oldest existing professional baseball park in US; the special game served as a tribute to the Negro Leagues of 1920s thru 1950s
Oldest deep-sea shipwreck ever discovered at 3,300 years old from the late Bronze Age, revealed off the northern coast of Israel carrying Canaanite amphorae
Battle of the Catalaunian Plains: Roman and Visigoth forces defeatAttila the Hunin northeast France, halting the Hun invasion of Roman Gaul [exact date disputed]
Foundation stone laid for Burgos Cathedral in the presence ofFerdinand III of Castile(made a UNESCO World Heritage Site 1984)
Diet Augsburg inaugurated by EmperorCharles Vto address Ottoman threat and rising Protestant tensions
Forces of Maurice and WilliamLouis of Nassauoccupy Dutch city of Deventer
Battle of Jajau: Bahadur Shah, son of recently deceased EmperorAurangzeb, defeats his brother Azam Shah, who is killed, to claim the Mughal throne
KingLouis XVIand the French royal family are caught during an attempt to flee Paris during the French Revolution, severely undermining their credibility
British GovernorStamford Rafflesstorms the kraton (palace) of Yogyakarta on Java, capturing its ruler Hamengkubuwono III
Coronation ofCharles X, the last Bourbon King of France
Queen Victoriaat 18 ascends British throne following death of uncle King William IV. She rules for 63 years till 1901.
American inventorSamuel Morsepatents his telegraph
PopeLeo XIIIpublishes encyclical Libertas
Lizzie Bordenacquitted of the 1892 axe murders of her father and stepmother in Fall River, Massachusetts
Canadian authorLucy Maud Montgomeryreceives her first copy of her novel "Anne of Green Gables" (published April 1908, a bestseller)
American sprinterJesse Owenssets a 100m world record of 10.2 seconds
Joe LouisTKOs Arturo Godoy in 8 for heavyweight boxing title
U.S. Secretary of State Edward Stettinius Jr. approves transfer of German rocket scientistWernher von Braunand his specialists to the US

"Toast of the Town" variety show hosted byEd Sullivanpremieres on CBS-TV; performers include the comedy team ofDean MartinandJerry Lewis, and Broadway composersRichard RodgersandOscar Hammerstein IIpreviewing the score to "South Pacific"
At Detroit's Briggs Stadium,Mickey Mantlehits 2 Billy Hoeft pitches into right center field bleachers, the only player to ever do so
"The Huckleberry Hound Show" byHanna-Barberabecomes the first animated programme to win an Emmy
12th Emmy Awards: Playhouse 90,Robert Stack&Jane Wyattwin
Floyd Pattersonbecomes first man to regain the world heavyweight boxing title when he KOsIngemar Johanssonin 5 in New York
PilotSheila Scott(43) completes 1st round-the-world solo flight by a British woman, returning to London's Heathrow airport in her Piper Comanche after a 34-day journey
Jim Hinesbecomes 1st person to run 100 meters in under 10 seconds
200,000 attend Newport '69, then largest-ever pop concert in Northridge, California.Jimi Hendrixpaid $120,000 to perform.
Georges Pompidousworn in as President of France
Oriole'sBrooks Robinsonget his 2,000 career hit, a 3 run HR
Juan Perónreturns from exile to Argentina after 18 years
SF GiantsBobby Bondssets NL record with 22 lead off HRs
"Chinatown", directed byRoman PolanskistarringJack NicholsonandFaye Dunaway, is released
Film "Jaws," based on the book byPeter Benchley, directed bySteven Spielbergand starring Roy Scheider, is released
Menachem Beginforms Israeli government
US PresidentJimmy Carterunveils 32 solar panels installed on the roof of the White House; his immediate successor has them removed
Musical comedy film "The Blues Brothers", starringDan AykroydandJohn Belushi, and featuring appearances byAretha Franklin;Cab Calloway; andRay Charlesopens in 594 theaters
Panamanian boxerRoberto Durántakes WBC welterweight title fromSugar Ray Leonardat Olympic Stadium in Montreal by unanimous points decision
PopeJohn Paul IIhospitalized for 55 days for infection
MLB Philadelphia PhilliesPete Rosebecomes 5th player to appear in 3,000 games, joining Ty Cobb, Stan Musial, Hank Aaron, and Carl Yastrzemski
New York Yankees 5-time MLB All Star outfielderBobby Murcerretires
Oakland A'sDave Kingmanhits his 3rd grand slam & 14th lifetime
Jim Fregosi replacesTony La Russaas White Sox manager
Nelson Mandelalands in NYC to begin a tour of US
Dino Ciccarelliis traded by the Washington Capitals to the Detroit Red Wings
Former NFL running back, broadcaster and actorO.J. Simpsonarraigned on murder of Nicole Simpson & Ronald Goldman
ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Lord's, London, England: Australia wins its second trophy with 8 wicket win over Pakistan; Player of the Match:Shane Warne(AUS) 4/33 (9)
Joel Embiidundergoes surgery on a broken navicular bone in his right foot
British actorDaniel Day-Lewisannounces his retirement from acting
Tiger Woodschecks into a clinic to manage his pain medication and sleep disorder, following his arrest for driving under the influence
TV drama "Yellowstone" premieres starringKevin Costner, Luke Grimes, Kelly Reilly andWes Bentley
US PresidentDonald Trumpsigns Executive Order ending family separation at the border for illegal immigrants
Chinese PresidentXi JinpingmeetsKim Jong-unin Pyongyang, the first Chinese president to visit North Korea in 14 years

David Gilmourauctions 126 of his guitars for record-breaking $21M USD, including “The Black Strat,” a 1969 Fender Stratocaster used onPink Floydalbums “The Dark Side of the Moon,” “Wish You Were Here.” and “The Wall” ($3.975M), at Christie's in New York City; charity auction proceeds earmarked to fight against climate change
Pharrell Williamsmakes his debut as menswear designer of fashion label Louis Vuitton staging his show on Paris' Port Neuf bridge
Site ofJulius Caesar's assassination in Rome, Largo Argentina square, dating back to third century BC, opens to the public for the first time
French Revolution: The Third Estate of the French Estates-General swears the Tennis Court Oath at the Palace of Versailles, vowing not to disband until a new constitution is formed
The SS Savannah reaches Cork in Ireland after a 29 day and 11 hour voyage from Savannah, Georgia to become the 1st steamship to cross the Atlantic or any other ocean
US PresidentAndrew Johnsonannounces the Alaska Purchase
Philipp Scheidemann resigns as chancellor of the new German Republic, which he helped establish, refusing to sign the Treaty of Versailles
Adolf Eichmannproclaims deportation of Dutch Jews
Tennis star Gussie Moran shocks Wimbledon by wearing a short dress "to look good and "move more freely on the court"
